name: Whole or Halved Tomatoes (Packed in Tomato Juice)
recipeIngredient: An average of 21 pounds is needed per canner load of 7 quarts.
An average of 13 pounds is needed per canner load of 9 pints.
A bushel weighs 53 pounds and yields 15 to 21 quarts, an average of 3 pounds per quart.
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash tomatoes thoroughly.
2. Dip each tomato in boiling water for 30 to 60 seconds or until the skins split. Immediately dip them in cold water.
3. Remove the skins by gently rubbing the tomatoes with your fingers. Cut out any damaged areas and cores.
4. Leave the tomatoes whole or halve them, if preferred.
5. Add bottled lemon juice or citric acid to each jar (see acidification instructions).
6. Stir 1 teaspoon of salt per quart into the jars, if desired.
7. For raw packing:
a. Heat tomato juice in a saucepan.
b. Fill jars with tomatoes, leaving ½-inch headspace.
c. Pour hot tomato juice over the tomatoes, maintaining ½-inch headspace.
8. For hot packing:
a. Place tomatoes and enough tomato juice to cover them in a large saucepan.
b. Heat gently for 5 minutes, boiling rapidly if needed.
c. Fill jars with hot tomatoes, leaving ½-inch headspace.
d. Pour hot tomato juice over the tomatoes, maintaining ½-inch headspace.
9. Process jars according to Table 1, Table 2 or Table 3 based on the chosen method:
- Table 1: Recommended process time for Whole or Halved Tomatoes (packed in tomato juice) using a boiling-water canner.
Style of Pack: Hot
Jar Size: Pints or Quarts
Process Time at Altitudes:
- 0 - 1,000 ft: 85 minutes
- 1,001 - 3,000 ft: 90 minutes
- 3,001 - 6,000 ft: 95 minutes
- Above 6,000 ft: 100 minutes
- Table 2: Recommended process time for Whole or Halved Tomatoes (packed in tomato juice) using a weighted-gauge pressure canner.
Style of Pack: Hot
Jar Size: Pints and Quarts
Process Time:
- Pints: 40 minutes at 5 PSI, increase to 10 PSI above 1,000 ft altitude.
- Quarts: 25 minutes at 10 PSI, increase to 15 PSI above 1,000 ft altitude.
- Table 3: Recommended process time for Whole or Halved Tomatoes (packed in tomato juice) using a dial-gauge pressure canner.
Style of Pack: Hot or Raw
Jar Size: Pints and Quarts
Process Time:
- Pints: 40 minutes at 6 PSI, increase to 9 PSI above 2,000 ft altitude.
- Quarts: 25 minutes at 11 PSI, increase to 14 PSI above 2,000 ft altitude.
